title: rubus × trilobatus
intro: Rubus × trilobatus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant species within the rose family, resulting from the cross between the red thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus) and the Pacific thimbleberry (Rubus laciniatus). The plant's medicinal properties lie in its high content of anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus × trilobatus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system, as well as its traditional use in treating digestive issues and fever. Herbal preparations of Rubus × trilobatus can be made into teas, tinctures, and jams, and it is also used in traditional medicine to make remedies for various ailments.
